item.title: Russia says internet outages to last as long as ‘necessary’
item.description: Mobile internet outages in Russia will last as long as ‘necessary’ to ensure citizens’ safety, the Kremlin said Wednesday, after network disruptions were recorded in Moscow and other Russian cities......Breaking news alerts

item.title: States backing UN peacekeepers in Lebanon voice ‘deep alarm’ at hostilities
item.description: Around 30 countries, including those with United Nations peacekeepers in Lebanon, voiced concern Wednesday over renewed fighting after Israel launched strikes in retaliation for attacks by Hezbollah......

item.title: Fuel pump owners seek army deployment amid long queues
item.description: Petrol pump owners in Bangladesh on Wednesday called for stronger security measures, including the deployment of army personnel, as long queues are formed at filling stations following the government’s decision to limit fuel sales nationwide due to the conflict in the Gulf region......

item.title: Iran hits ships in Gulf chokepoint
item.description: Iran fired on two commercial ships in the Gulf on Wednesday, setting at least one vessel on fire, as it pressed its campaign against its energy-exporting neighbours and forced world governments to dip into their strategic oil reserves......

item.title: Gulf conflicts may weigh heavily on budget
item.description: The escalating conflicts in the Gulf region causing volatility in prices of energy items would put further pressure on the fiscal side, officials said on Wednesday......News website membership

item.title: US intel report finds N Korean cyber group involved: CID
item.description: The Criminal Investigation Department has received an intelligence report from the United States that found that Park Jin Hyok, a member of North Korean state-sponsored cyber group named Lazarus Group, was behind the 2016 cyber heist from the Bangladesh Bank’s foreign reserves......

item.title: PM urges cleanliness drive to prevent dengue, chikungunya
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Wednesday called upon the people of the country to take preventive measures against dengue and chikungunya, announcing that a nationwide cleanliness campaign would begin from March 14 to help protect citizens from the mosquito-borne diseases......

item.title: Diesel shortage disrupts boro irrigation
item.description: Farmers in several districts are struggling to irrigate boro paddy fields amid a diesel shortage, raising concerns among agriculture economists about the country’s rice supply in the coming months......

item.title: Flipped classroom: beyond the lecture
item.description: THE image of a classroom has not changed in Bangladesh over decades. A teacher occupies the front, delivers a lecture and students listen to the teacher or fall asleep. After that, the students take assignments home to complete individually. Such an approach to teaching traditionally results in boredom, memorising and de-motivation. But what would happen if the......Breaking news alerts

item.title: Do they add anything?
item.description: IN THE long, and often turbulent,politicalhistory, a few politicians have elicited the level of emotional attachment and symbolism that surrounded the life of Khaleda Zia, who was the chairperson of the Bangladesh Nationbalist Party. She had not merely been a politician for decades. She had also been a symbol of defiance, perseverance and political conviction. People......

item.title: Reform talk far from market queue
item.description: EVERY Ramadan, a familiar ritual unfolds in Bangladesh’s television studios and public discussions. Economists, business leaders, political figures and policy analysts gather to talk about inflation, market volatility and the broader direction of the economy. The questions are predictable: how severe are the price hikes, how are markets performing and how are people......

item.title: A kidney care roadmap
item.description: THE recent election has ushered in a renewed sense of hope across Bangladesh. With a fresh mandate, the newly elected government now has a rare, golden opportunity to overhaul the systemic failures plaguing our healthcare sector. Among the myriad of urgent priorities, one silent crisis demands immediate, decisive action: the management of kidney failure......

item.title: Reclaiming educational philosophy
item.description: BANGLADESH’S education system stands at a critical moment. Over the past several decades, the country has expanded access to schooling at a pace that once seemed difficult to imagine. Enrolment has increased significantly, gender parity has improved, and schooling is now far more accessible across rural and urban areas. Yet alongside these achievements......

item.title: No compromise on education governance
item.description: THE education ministry’s plan to relax or revoke the requirement that chair of governing bodies of non-government educational institutions should have at least a bachelor’s degree raises serious concerns about academic governance. The ministry held a meeting on the reformation of the governing bodies and managing committees of non-government educational......

item.title: Govt must accord grandeur to Independence Day
item.description: SHOPPING malls and markets continue to be illuminated on the occasion of Eid-ul-Fitr, which will be celebrated in little more than a week, as much in Dhaka as in outlying areas to draw in buyers. Some of the malls in some places are illuminated even during the daytime. Much of it has been an instance of competition between neighbouring shopping malls as no one wants to......

item.title: PSG hit five to stun Chelsea in first leg
item.description: A gift by Chelsea goalkeeper Filip Jorgensen and two late goals by Khvicha Kvaratskhelia gave title holders Paris Saint-Germain a stunning 5-2 win over the English side in a thrilling Champions League last 16, first leg on Wednesday....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Real Madrid run riot as Valverde treble stuns City
item.description:
Federico Valverde's superb first-half hat-trick helped Real Madrid demolish Manchester City 3-0 in a surprise Champions League last 16 first leg rout on Wednesday.
...

item.title: Inaugural Women’s BPL deferred to July
item.description: The proposed inaugural edition of the Women’s Bangladesh Premier League has been deferred from April to July, confirmed Bangladesh Cricket Board on Wednesday. ...Breaking news alerts

item.title: DU students stage Tabula Rasa
item.description: The students of the Department of Theatre and Performance Studies at Dhaka University staged a theatre production titled ‘Tabula Rasa’ at the university's Natmandal on Wednesday......

item.title: Translations on high demand
item.description: Publishers, stall managers, and translators at the Amar Ekushey Book Fair 2026 observed on Wednesday that translations drove the country’s publishing industry and those were on high demand throughout the year......

item.title: 20 DU students get Jagannath Hall Trust Fund
item.description: Twenty students from different departments, institutes and years of Dhaka University have been awarded Jagannath Hall Trust Fund scholarships for their outstanding academic achievements......

item.title: 10 upazilas finalised for piloting Krishak Card programme
item.description: The agriculture ministry has finalised 10 upazilas to pilot the Krishak Card programme for 180 days beginning in mid-April......News website membership

item.title: Further probe report submission deferred for 8th time
item.description: An additional chief metropolitan magistrate court in Dhaka on Wednesday extended the time for submitting a further investigation report in the case of the killing of Inquilab Mancha convener Sharif Osman Hadi again by 22 more days......
